name: Identity & PAM — Entra ID + Okta + CyberArk
description: ATT&CK technique coverage for identity and privileged access management vendors: Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, and CyberArk. Coverage via NIST 800-53 controls IA-2, IA-5, AC-2, AC-3, AC-6, IA-4. Source: https://github.com/TeamStarWolf/TeamStarWolf/blob/main/data/vendor_to_technique.jsonl

techniques.comment: Entra ID + Okta — IA-2/AC-2: MFA, Conditional Access, and anomalous sign-in detection prevent/detect valid account abuse
Entra ID + Okta — Domain Accounts: privileged identity protection
Entra ID + Okta — Cloud Accounts: cloud identity anomaly detection
Entra ID + Okta — IA-2/IA-5: MFA and smart lockout prevent brute force attacks
Entra ID + Okta — Password Guessing prevention via lockout + MFA
Entra ID + Okta — Password Spraying detection and throttling
Entra ID + Okta — Credential Stuffing prevention via MFA
Entra ID — IA-2: Conditional Access policies detect attempts to modify authentication process
Entra ID — Domain Controller Authentication modification detection
Entra ID — Multi-Factor Authentication modification detection
Entra ID + Okta — IA-2: MFA fatigue/push bombing detection and number matching enforcement
Entra ID + Okta — AC-2: account manipulation alerts (new admin role, MFA changes, added credentials)
Entra ID — Additional Cloud Credentials detection
Entra ID — Additional Cloud Roles detection
Entra ID — Device Registration detection
Entra ID + Okta — AC-2: unauthorized account creation alerting and prevention
Entra ID — Domain Account creation detection
Entra ID — Cloud Account creation detection
CyberArk — AC-6: PAM vaulting prevents credential dumping by blocking access to privileged account passwords
CyberArk — LSASS Memory protection via privileged session isolation
CyberArk — AC-6: least privilege enforcement limits exploitation for privilege escalation
CyberArk — AC-6: Local Accounts vaulted and rotated, blocking lateral movement via local admin
CyberArk — Pass-the-Hash prevention via privileged account isolation and rotation
CyberArk — AC-6: Kerberoasting mitigation via managed service accounts with rotating passwords
CyberArk — Kerberoasting: managed service account password rotation

legendItems.label: Covered by Entra ID / Okta / CyberArk

metadata.name: Primary Controls
metadata.value: IA-2, IA-5, AC-2, AC-3, AC-6, IA-4
